trajectory_summary: A warm, motivated lead with a known site complexity — tree coverage on the satellite image — that Parker never got past. The call's energy was present at the open (Johnny volunteered enthusiasm and a $2,300 bill unprompted) but leaked steadily as the tree logistics consumed every available minute. No verify items were collected, the bill was not acknowledged or flipped, and the call ended with a loose photo-and-text plan with no locked callback time. The outcome is a conditional non-booking: Parker has a follow-up hook but no appointment structure.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is unknown, but this call should be reviewed as a missed booking rather than a successful set. No button_up elements were established — no appointment datetime, no one-hour block, no computer/no-driving anchor, no 'is that fair' handshake. The follow-up plan ('figure out a time over text') is a soft callback with no locked time, which the schema distinguishes as a materially lower-value outcome than a booked sit.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Locking a callback time — converting a soft follow-up into a booked touchpoint
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Yeah. Just send me a photo when you can. We'll start on our end, and then we can kinda figure out a time over text. Would that work for you?
primary_coaching_focus.why: Parker had a warm, motivated customer who volunteered a $2,300 bill, committed three times to removing tree obstructions, and closed by saying 'come any time you want.' The call ended with zero button_up elements and a fully open-ended follow-up. The photo logistics are a real constraint, but they don't prevent Parker from locking a specific callback time — 'I'll call you at 5pm once you've sent the photo.' That single move converts a loose callback into a trackable, show-rate-relevant touchpoint. This is the structural gap that puts the sit at risk regardless of how warm the lead is.

key_moments.quote: hoping y'all come and put it in.
key_moments.stewart_read: Johnny opens with unprompted readiness — he's not waiting to be persuaded, he's expressing a desire for the install to happen. This is the strongest possible starting signal for a setter call. Parker acknowledges it briefly ('We wanna come show you the design') but does not anchor on it or use it to build momentum into the verify section.

key_moments.quote: yeah. I can. And, like I said, I have no problem on cutting them down either, anything in the day.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the moment that dissolves the tree-obstruction barrier. Johnny is not just saying the trees can come down — he is saying he will do it proactively, on any timeline, to make the install happen. Parker hears this and continues asking clarifying questions about the trees rather than pivoting to 'great, so the site works — let's get you on the calendar.' The barrier Parker has been treating as a blocker is explicitly removed here.

key_moments.quote: right now, my life, the electric company went crazy. They sent me a $2,300.
key_moments.stewart_read: Johnny volunteers a $2,300 bill mid-conversation — unprompted, emotionally loaded ('the electric company went crazy'). This is the bill anchor the schema says must be captured AND flipped within 4 minutes. Parker's response is a third clarifying question about the trees ('So you said there are there some trees already cut down...'). The bill is not acknowledged, not flipped, and not returned to for the remainder of the call. The failure here is not that the bill wasn't captured as a data point — Parker heard it — but that there was no acknowledgment and no flip into emotional alignment-against-the-utility.

key_moments.quote: Well, right now I'm training here, but I'm thinking I'll try to get you one and
key_moments.stewart_read: Johnny discloses he is currently in a training session — a real-time availability signal. Parker acknowledges it implicitly ('Okay. Gotcha.') but does not use it to anchor a specific callback time. 'I'm training right now but I'll get you a photo later today' is a natural opening to say: 'Perfect — so you'll be free around [time]? Let me lock that in as our callback.' Parker lets it pass as a logistics note rather than a scheduling anchor.
